title: Senior Product Manager, Claude
score_rationale: Strong AI/ML domain alignment — your search platform experience maps directly to building LLM-powered products. Samsung and Amazon search roles show exactly the data-driven PM profile Anthropic looks for. Minor gap: no direct safety/alignment background, but quantified ML impact covers 80% of requirements.
recommendation_reason: Top-tier AI company with direct domain match; your search + ML PM background is a strong fit for Claude product work.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with 7+ years leading AI/ML-powered search and discovery products at scale (10M+ MAU, $2B+ GMV impact). Track record shipping data-driven features that measurably improve user outcomes through ML and experimentation.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search platform serving 10M+ monthly users, improving CTR by 23%
$2B+ GMV search relevance at Amazon, shipped with cross-functional ML teams
Built real-time personalization engine increasing conversion by 18%
gap_handling: Acknowledge no direct safety research background; emphasize genuine interest in responsible AI and frame LLM product work as adjacent context.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's profile is an exceptionally strong match for this role: 9 years of PM experience directly covering AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and recommendation systems — all core to Airbnb's homepage transformation. The Samsung.com work (hybrid AI search, conversational AI, behavioral ranking, 10M+ monthly users) and Kolon e-commerce discovery work map almost 1:1 to the JD requirements. The only meaningful gaps are consumer travel/marketplace domain exposure and the emphasis on lifecycle/growth marketing partnership, which is a secondary but notable requirement Riley has some but not deep coverage on. Seniority is a slight stretch — Airbnb's 8+ year bar with executive-facing ownership signals a Lead/Staff-tier expectation, one notch above Riley's target level.
recommendation_reason: Core discovery + AI search background is a near-perfect domain match; strong quantified evidence across scale metrics (10M+ users, CTR gains, ranking precision) makes Riley a compelling applicant despite the slight seniority stretch.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered search, discovery, and personalization experiences at scale — from hybrid search ranking for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com to ML-driven recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV e-commerce platform. Proven track record of shipping consumer discovery products that drive measurable engagement, conversion, and growth in partnership with design, engineering, data science, and marketing teams.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 → $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The role has a meaningful lifecycle/growth marketing partnership dimension (extending discovery into CRM campaigns). Riley should surface the CDP + CRM personalization work from Kolon (50% CTR lift, 45% marketing ROI increase) and Samsung's behavioral insights-to-engagement loop to demonstrate cross-channel discovery thinking. Acknowledge in interviews that consumer travel marketplace is a new domain, but anchor on transferable discovery and ranking expertise.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background — particularly conversational AI, LLM-driven pipelines, and search/discovery systems at Samsung — maps well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role, which is deeply AI-native. The gap is that Klaviyo wants B2B SaaS / brand-facing platform PM experience (configuring AI agents for business customers), whereas Riley's AI work has been primarily B2C consumer-facing; the Amazon B2B role bridges this partially but lacked AI depth. RAG/agent architecture fluency and zero-to-one B2B SaaS ownership are mentioned as important and are undersold in the resume, even if the underlying experience exists. The role is Boston-based with no remote mention, which is a mild friction point given Riley's Bellevue location.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML product credentials and conversational AI experience are directly relevant, but the B2B SaaS platform PM angle and Boston location create real friction worth weighing before applying.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-native products — from conversational AI chat experiences and LLM-driven pipelines to hybrid search and personalization systems at scale. Proven track record leading zero-to-one AI product builds end-to-end, partnering with ML and data science teams to translate complex AI systems into intuitive, high-impact customer experiences for millions of users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The role heavily emphasizes B2B SaaS platform PM work — specifically designing brand-facing agent configuration systems, onboarding flows, and activation funnels for business customers. Riley should explicitly reframe the Amazon B2B listing tool work as a configuration/onboarding platform PM story, and call out any agent/LLM architecture knowledge (RAG, tool-calling, retrieval systems) in a skills section or summary. If applying, a cover note addressing the location situation (willing to relocate or work hybrid) would meaningfully strengthen the application.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's core expertise in AI-powered search, ranking algorithms, relevance metrics, and experimentation maps directly onto Faire's search algorithms mandate — this is about as domain-aligned as it gets. However, the role is titled Staff PM and explicitly requires 7+ years at a high-growth tech startup or as a startup founder, with deep involvement in "state-of-the-art engineering and data science" at scale; Riley's B2C retail search experience (Samsung.com) and Amazon platform background are strong, but the B2B wholesale marketplace context and the Staff-level seniority bar create a meaningful stretch. The hybrid NYC/SF requirement is also a location friction point given Riley is based in Bellevue, WA.
recommendation_reason: Excellent domain and skills alignment on search algorithms/AI, but the Staff PM seniority bar and NYC/SF hybrid requirement are real hurdles for a Bellevue-based Senior/PM III candidate
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience owning search algorithms, ranking systems, and discovery surfaces at scale — including hybrid AI search for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven catalog systems for 2M+ sellers at Amazon. Proven track record defining relevance metrics, running experimentation programs, and shipping state-of-the-art search products end-to-end with data science and engineering partners.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and 7+ years at a "high-growth tech startup" bar is the sharpest gap. Riley's Samsung tenure is at a large enterprise, not a classic startup. To address this in the application, lean into the Amazon experience (Square-DNA company, startup-scale velocity) and frame the Samsung role as operating with startup-like ownership over a greenfield search product. If applying, consider a cover letter that explicitly calls out search algorithm depth and the CEO/founder-level decision-making analogy from her cross-org executive alignment work.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: Remote-Friendly (Travel-Required) | San Francisco, CA | Seattle, WA; San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing fluency, and measurable growth metrics (CTR, AOV, conversion) align well with Anthropic's data-driven growth mandate. However, the role explicitly requires "majority in growth focused roles" for developer/API/platform products — Riley's growth work has been consumer e-commerce and B2C discovery, not developer tooling, API funnels, or B2B SaaS growth. The Amazon B2B listing tool is the closest bridge, but it's platform tooling rather than API-developer growth. The Anthropic brand, compensation ($305K–$385K), and visa sponsorship commitment are outstanding tailwinds.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/growth credentials and explicit visa sponsorship, but the developer/API platform growth focus is a meaningful gap vs. Riley's consumer e-commerce background.
suggested_summary: Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered growth across acquisition, activation, and monetization — shipping hybrid search, recommendation systems, and conversational AI experiences that measurably moved the needle for 10M+ monthly users. Experienced partnering with ML engineers and data scientists to instrument funnels, run A/B experiments, and optimize end-to-end product experiences on consumer and B2B platforms including Amazon and Samsung.com.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — demonstrating platform growth via self-serve, onboarding optimization, and funnel instrumentation.

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics; increased search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ, serving 10M+ monthly users.
Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data — bridging technical API delivery with measurable growth outcomes (AOV +15%).
gap_handling: The primary gap is developer/API platform growth experience (dev onboarding, console UX, billing, self-serve upgrade paths). In the cover letter, bridge this by leading with the Amazon B2B seller platform (self-serve workflow, onboarding optimization, ML-driven automation for a technical user base) and the recommendation API and AEO pipeline work at Samsung as evidence of API-layer product thinking. Frame the shift from consumer growth to developer growth as deliberate and natural given Riley's AI/ML foundation.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background — especially conversational AI, LLM pipelines, RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, hybrid search), and behavioral data instrumentation — maps well to Twilio's Copilot backend vision. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata extraction pipeline are the strongest direct evidence signals. However, the role is titled "Principal PM" and requires 8+ years with a clear backend services / developer platform / B2B SaaS focus, which is a meaningful gap: Riley's background skews consumer/e-commerce rather than CPaaS or developer tooling, and lacks explicit RAG pipeline ownership, support ticket deflection, or multi-tenant B2B platform experience at the level Twilio expects.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM credentials and conversational AI delivery experience bridge the gap, but the Principal-level seniority and developer platform domain are a stretch from Riley's e-commerce/consumer PM background.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping production LLM systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to automated metadata pipelines — at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record building backend AI orchestration and retrieval systems that drive measurable engagement, deflection, and conversion at scale for millions of users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: Twilio will probe hard on RAG pipeline design, developer persona experience, and B2B SaaS platform thinking. Prepare concrete talking points around your AEO pipeline (position it as a knowledge-extraction and structured-retrieval system analogous to RAG), your conversational AI architecture decisions, and your Amazon B2B platform work. Acknowledge the CPaaS domain gap directly but bridge it: you've built AI systems that serve complex product catalogs at scale — the same contextual-data-to-agent pattern Twilio needs.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Features
score_rationale: Good AI domain alignment and remote flexibility. Figma's AI features team is building generative design tools — your ML product experience is relevant, but design tool domain is new territory. Staff-level role is a stretch given target Senior PM level.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI PM match, but design domain is unfamiliar and Staff level is a stretch — worth applying if you can connect to design workflow context.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with deep AI/ML product delivery experience and a track record of leading cross-functional teams to ship data-driven features. Excited to bring search + personalization expertise to AI-powered creative tooling.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search platform serving 10M+ monthly users, improving CTR by 23%
Partnered with data science teams to build real-time recommendation systems
Drove A/B experimentation framework reducing feature release cycle by 40%
gap_handling: Design tool domain gap is real — acknowledge it, but pivot to how ML/search PM skills (user behavior signals, relevance, recommendations) transfer directly to AI feature work in any domain.

title: Senior Product Manager, Notion AI
score_rationale: Notion AI is building LLM-powered writing and knowledge management features. Your AI/ML product experience is directionally right, but consumer productivity SaaS is a different motion than ecommerce search. NY location is a match. Score reflects domain gap rather than skill gap.
recommendation_reason: Good AI fit and NYC location match, but consumer SaaS / productivity domain is new — tailor your application to highlight user behavior and growth metrics.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with 7+ years shipping AI/ML-powered products at scale. Experience using quantitative signals and experimentation to drive discovery and engagement — skills that transfer directly to AI-powered knowledge tools.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search and discovery platform serving 10M+ monthly users
Drove AEO strategy improving organic discovery by 35%
Built personalization engine increasing conversion rate by 18%
gap_handling: Consumer SaaS / productivity domain is less familiar than ecommerce — frame your experience around user intent, discovery, and engagement patterns, which are common threads.

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, agentic workflows (AEO), and conversational AI — that directly map to Claude Code's frontier AI DNA. However, the role has a hard requirement of 1+ year as a professional software engineer, and there is no engineering experience on Riley's resume; the background is squarely PM/business analytics. Claude Code is also a developer tools / CLI product aimed at software engineers, which is outside Riley's e-commerce search and discovery domain, creating a meaningful archetype gap.
recommendation_reason: Riley's AI product depth and LLM pipeline experience are compelling, but the mandatory engineering background and dev-tools domain are genuine gaps that make this a reach without strong framing.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience shipping LLM-driven pipelines, agentic workflows, and AI-powered experiences at scale — including conversational AI, hybrid search systems, and automated metadata extraction pipelines. Deeply hands-on with frontier AI capabilities and model evaluation, with a track record of translating cutting-edge research into high-impact, production-ready products used by millions of users.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: The engineering background requirement is the biggest risk. Riley should address it directly in the cover letter by highlighting hands-on technical depth: SQL/BigQuery work, close partnership with ML engineers defining model evaluation metrics (precision@3), architecting multi-modal classification pipelines at Amazon, and prompt engineering / LLM evaluation experience. Frame the MS in Business Analytics and Information Management (Purdue) as a technical graduate credential. If Riley has any personal coding projects, side tools, or experience using Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot, mention that explicitly — this role values a "hacker spirit."

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product depth, search and discovery expertise, and measurable consumer impact at Samsung and Kolon are strong signals — and the domain alignment with consumer AI is excellent. However, Anthropic is explicitly seeking a "former consumer founder" with deep 0-to-1 product creation chops and the ability to prototype directly with tools like Claude Code; Riley's resume is execution- and optimization-heavy rather than 0-to-1 launch-heavy, and there's no founder background. The technical bar here also skews toward hands-on prototyping and direct research collaboration at a frontier AI lab, which is a meaningful gap compared to Riley's current profile.
recommendation_reason: Exceptional domain match and AI product credibility, but the founder/0-to-1 requirement and hands-on prototyping bar are significant gaps that make this a competitive stretch.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years launching consumer-facing AI/ML products at scale — including hybrid search, conversational AI, and personalization systems serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and $2B+ GMV platforms. Deep track record of leading ambiguous, first-of-kind AI initiatives from 0 to production, partnering directly with ML researchers and engineers to ship experiences that drive measurable consumer behavior change.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: The role explicitly asks for a "former consumer founder" — if Riley has any entrepreneurial experience (side projects, startup stints, internal 0-to-1 initiatives that mirror founding), those should be surfaced prominently. Lean into the AEO pipeline and conversational AI launches as the closest 0-to-1 analogues. Also consider adding a line about hands-on AI prototyping (e.g., using Claude or similar tools to build MVPs) to directly address the "Prototype with AI tools like Claude Code" signal.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product chops and solid A/B testing and analytics credentials, which align well with Anthropic's technical culture and data-driven growth expectations. However, the role is specifically a **Monetization PM** — demanding direct ownership of subscription pricing, packaging, free-to-paid conversion funnels, and consumption-based billing — and Riley's resume doesn't prominently feature these levers. The closest signals are AOV lifts and onboarding/retention work at Kolonmall and the Amazon B2B onboarding flow, but none of these frame Riley as a monetization specialist. The PM archetype here is Growth/Monetization, not AI Search/Discovery, which is Riley's primary strength.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and analytical foundation, but the role demands dedicated monetization/subscription growth experience that isn't front-and-center in Riley's resume — significant tailoring required to be competitive.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth through data-driven experimentation, behavioral optimization, and AI-powered product experiences at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. Proven track record of improving conversion, retention, and revenue metrics at scale — including AOV growth on a $2B GMV platform — with deep experience collaborating cross-functionally on fast-moving product and growth initiatives.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program and boosted 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for B2B sellers that reduced manual contacts with support agents, identifying key issues via 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys
gap_handling: Riley lacks explicit monetization framing (no owned pricing strategy, subscription tier design, or free-to-paid funnel ownership). To close this gap: (1) Reframe the Kolonmall AOV and retention bullets to explicitly call out "revenue growth" and "conversion funnel monetization." (2) If there's any pricing, packaging, or upsell work done at Samsung.com (e.g., the add-ons page / AOV boost), elevate that prominently. (3) Consider a brief cover letter note acknowledging the growth/monetization angle and tying AI product experience to Anthropic's Claude.ai subscription context.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, SQL/Tableau tooling, and experience with agentic/LLM pipelines (AEO pipeline, conversational AI) are meaningful overlaps with this role. However, this is a Staff-level Platform PM role focused on internal AI infrastructure and marketplace systems at a ride-sharing company — a domain and archetype that diverge from Riley's consumer e-commerce search background. The explicit requirements around agentic systems, multi-agent orchestration, internal platform adoption/evangelism, CXO-level presentations, and SaaS vendor/contract management are only partially evidenced in the resume, and the "marketplace" context here is ride-sharing, not e-commerce.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML credentials and tooling overlap, but Staff-level stretch, platform PM archetype mismatch, and ride-sharing marketplace domain gap make this a competitive long-shot requiring significant tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving end-to-end AI/ML systems — from LLM-powered pipelines and agentic workflows to search ranking and personalization platforms — that serve millions of users and generate measurable business outcomes. Proven track record partnering with data scientists and engineers to build and ship AI platform capabilities, define KPIs, and scale adoption across cross-functional organizations.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams, leveraging behavioral insights to iterate search experiences and increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: The biggest gaps are (1) internal platform / developer-tools PM experience — Riley has built products *using* AI but hasn't owned an AI platform consumed by other internal teams; and (2) explicit agentic/multi-agent orchestration work. To bridge this, Riley should reframe the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as platform-building efforts with internal stakeholder adoption, and explicitly call out any multi-agent or orchestration components of those systems. Adding even one line about coordinating with internal teams as "platform customers" would strengthen the narrative considerably.

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops, SQL fluency, LLM/conversational AI experience, and A/B testing rigor are strong matches for this role's technical bar. However, the role is squarely in the enterprise B2B SaaS / developer tooling space (observability + BI/analytics) — a domain Riley has not worked in — and requires deep knowledge of the modern data stack (semantic layers, data warehouses, BI tools like Looker/dbt/Snowflake), which doesn't appear in the resume. The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial framing and B2B SaaS requirement (5+ years) is also a partial gap, as Riley's B2B experience is primarily one year at Amazon (seller tools), with the bulk of experience in consumer e-commerce.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and analytical foundation, but missing the B2B SaaS depth and modern data stack domain knowledge this role explicitly requires — apply with a targeted narrative bridging the gap.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered experiences — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and personalization at scale — serving 10M+ users across Samsung.com and Amazon. Proven track record defining governance frameworks, instrumentation strategies, and eval-driven iteration loops for non-deterministic AI systems, with strong SQL fluency and cross-functional leadership across data science, engineering, and executive stakeholders.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Acknowledge the BI/data stack domain gap directly in your cover letter — frame your AEO structured data pipeline and conversational AI work as evidence you can reason about context layers and governance for AI systems. Reference familiarity with dbt/Snowflake/Looker concepts if you have any, even casually. The 0-to-1 framing is a risk; lean hard on your Samsung search product vision ownership and the fact that you defined the roadmap from scratch for a 10M+ user surface.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product experience — LLMs, conversational AI, agentic pipelines (AEO), and cross-functional delivery — which partially maps to Okta's AI Agents product. However, the role's core requirement is deep Identity and Access Management (IAM) expertise: OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, delegated authority, and token exchange protocols. There is no evidence of IAM, enterprise security, or developer-platform experience in Riley's resume. The domain is enterprise security/identity infrastructure, which is a significant departure from Riley's e-commerce and search background — this is the critical gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product chops are relevant, but missing IAM/OAuth/enterprise security experience is a near-disqualifying gap for the core requirements.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — including conversational AI agents, automated agentic pipelines, and multi-modal ML products — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Passionate about the intersection of AI and secure infrastructure, with a track record of translating complex technical architectures into measurable product outcomes for 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%; partnered with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Be upfront that your IAM depth is emerging, not established. Compensate by leaning into your LLM pipeline work (AEO, conversational AI), your experience defining agent-like automated workflows, and your pattern of shipping technically complex AI products. Consider self-studying OAuth2/OIDC fundamentals and referencing it in a cover letter — Okta will test for this in interviews. Frame the AEO pipeline as "structured agentic data infrastructure" to draw the closest analogy available.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's background in AI-powered search, discovery, and growth aligns meaningfully with Squarespace's Help Center redesign — particularly the AI chat, personalization, and deflection-rate angles. However, the core of this role is customer support infrastructure and help content platform management, which is largely absent from Riley's resume. The NYC hybrid requirement is also a location mismatch from Riley's Bellevue, WA base, and sponsorship is unconfirmed.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/discovery overlap but significant gap in support tooling and help platform domain; NYC hybrid requirement is a real friction point for a Bellevue-based candidate.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered discovery and conversational experiences at scale — including an on-site AI chat launch and hybrid search redesign serving 10M+ monthly users — with a consistent track record of turning complex, cross-functional platforms into measurable growth levers. Adept at leading empowered teams through blue-sky redesigns and leveraging AI to personalize and scale user-facing experiences, from search surfaces to guided shopping flows.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows
gap_handling: Riley lacks direct experience with customer support platforms, knowledge bases, CustOps tooling, and help deflection metrics — the core muscle this role requires. Frame the AEO pipeline and structured content work as analogous to knowledge base architecture. Be honest in interviews that support infrastructure is new territory, but position it as a deliberate scope expansion built on proven adjacent skills.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product experience — LLMs, conversational AI, agentic pipelines, and search — that partially satisfies Okta's demand for someone who's built with LLM-based systems. However, the role's core requirement is deep Identity and Access Management (IAM) expertise — OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, token exchange, agent authentication/authorization — which is essentially absent from Riley's resume. This is a developer-platform / security-infrastructure role, not an AI search or discovery role, creating a significant domain gap. The "Zero to One" product-building preferred experience is actually a good fit signal, and the AEO pipeline + conversational AI work provides some relevant AI agent adjacency.
recommendation_reason: Compelling AI/LLM product background checks a key box, but missing IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security platform experience is a hard gap the hiring team will notice immediately.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building LLM-powered and agentic product experiences — from conversational AI and AEO pipelines to hybrid search and multi-agent workflows — at enterprise scale (10M+ monthly users). Proven track record of shipping AI-native products end-to-end in cross-functional environments, with deep experience defining metrics, managing technical stakeholders, and driving Zero-to-One launches in fast-moving AI landscapes.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Be upfront about the IAM gap in a cover letter — frame it as intentional: "I bring the AI-native product depth; I'm actively deepening my understanding of OAuth2/OIDC in the context of non-human identity." Consider completing a short Okta Developer course or Auth0 tutorial and mentioning it. Lean hard into the agentic AI angle (AEO pipeline, conversational AI, multi-agent workflow familiarity) to earn the interview, then study IAM fundamentals intensively before any screen.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley has strong PM fundamentals — cross-functional leadership, A/B testing, data-driven roadmapping, and enterprise-scale execution — that satisfy Asana's seniority bar. However, the core domain mismatch is significant: this role is squarely in B2B SaaS work management (project/task UX, complex data models, enterprise compliance workflows), whereas Riley's background is almost entirely in e-commerce search, discovery, and AI-powered shopping experiences. The "curiosity about AI tools" signal is a nice-to-have rather than a core requirement, limiting the relevance of Riley's strongest AI/ML credentials. Riley's Amazon B2B platform experience is the closest analog, but it's a catalog/listing tool rather than productivity or collaboration software.
recommendation_reason: Strong PM fundamentals and B2B credibility from Amazon, but domain mismatch (work management vs. e-commerce/search) and NYC in-office requirement are meaningful hurdles for a Bellevue-based candidate.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building data-driven, AI-powered product experiences at enterprise scale — including B2B platform work at Amazon serving 2M+ sellers and search/discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung. Known for translating ambiguous, complex problems into clear roadmaps, partnering deeply with engineering and design, and shipping high-velocity iterative solutions that drive measurable business impact.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing a self-serve workflow for B2B sellers, identifying key issues via 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys and partnering with 5 dependent teams to deliver a scalable solution
gap_handling: Acknowledge the domain shift directly in a cover letter: frame your e-commerce and B2B platform work as evidence of building intuitive, high-complexity product experiences at scale, and position your Amazon listing tool work as the closest parallel to Asana's enterprise task/data-model domain. Avoid over-indexing on AI/search credentials — lean into cross-functional execution, enterprise UX, and roadmap leadership instead.

title: Senior Product Manager, APM - Observability
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths are in AI-powered search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization — domains that are largely orthogonal to Observability/APM. The role specifically requires hands-on APM, distributed tracing, Synthetics, and RUM experience, which are absent from Riley's resume. While there is transferable skill in working with ML engineers, data pipelines, and cross-functional delivery, none of Riley's bullets directly evidence monitoring, tracing, or developer tooling product experience.
recommendation_reason: APM/distributed tracing/Synthetics/RUM domain experience is a hard requirement Riley does not possess — the gap is too fundamental to paper over with tailoring.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered platform experiences at Samsung and Amazon, with deep expertise in search relevance, data pipelines, and instrumentation strategy. Experienced in defining success metrics, leading cross-functional engineering collaborations, and shipping ML-driven features at scale — currently expanding into observability and developer tooling domains.
top_bullets: Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams — increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: If Riley is genuinely interested in pivoting into Observability, the honest path is to gain foundational exposure first — e.g., contribute to open-source OpenTelemetry tooling, complete an APM/Observability certification (Elastic itself offers training), or target a bridge role in developer-facing platform PM closer to her current skillset. Applying now without any APM signal on the resume is unlikely to clear the recruiter screen.
